name: restful-booker
description: mwinteringham's restful-booker-platform (https://automationintesting.online, source https://github.com/mwinteringham/restful-booker-platform), as the qa-framework's SECOND site. Proves the engine is genuinely domain-agnostic: nothing in engine/ or policies/ changes between this and mock-shop. Ships a zero-dependency in-process mock for the deterministic offline gate AND a 'live' env pointing at the real public demo.

source.note: The in-repo mock IS the source (declared ROUTES + BUSINESS_RULES read by design + diagnostics), faithful to the real platform. For a real backend this would point at a checked-out clone of the GitHub source.

ui.note: The site serves a web UI here (relative to the runtime base_url). The mock serves a minimal booking form at /ui for the deterministic offline Playwright sample; the live site's UI is at the host root. UICases (ui-packs/) drive this with a real browser.

runtime.note: Default boots the mock in-process (deterministic, offline). 'isolate' clears ephemeral bookings before each case (a mock-only test seam; harmlessly 404s against the live site, where new_user cases self-clean via teardown). The 'live' env (below) overrides mode to 'remote'.

live.note: The real public demo API (served under /api). Use this env to exercise the REMOTE + login seam against the real site: run with QAF_CREDS_MODE=provider QAF_USERNAME=admin QAF_PASSWORD=password so plugin.py:resolve_creds logs in (POST /api/auth/login -> token). Scope: the offline mock is the deterministic gate; against the live site the behavioural rules the real platform implements hold (e.g. BOOK-3's 409 on overlapping dates), while packs asserting the mock's computed price fields (subtotal/discount/totalprice — the real booking model has none) are mock-scoped. e.g. QAF_ENV=live QAF_CREDS_MODE=provider QAF_USERNAME=admin QAF_PASSWORD=password make test SUT=sut/restful-booker

creds.note: The mock enforces no auth, so the offline gate is dependency-free. Override per-run with QAF_CREDS_MODE=provider to exercise the real cookie-login flow (plugin.py:resolve_creds) against the 'live' env.
